title: nezahualcoyotlia gracilis
intro: Nezahualcoyotlita gracilis is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, also known as the "Mexican tea" or "Mexican willow," belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Nezahualcoyotlita gracilis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting digestive health, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Nezahualcoyotlita gracilis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
